etymology.description: 又(又) (yòu, "hand") + 𰀁 (brush) = hand holding a brush -> writing brush -> borrowed to represent 聿 (yù, "thereupon")
etymology.interpretation: The character 聿 originally depicted a hand grasping a brush, symbolizing the act of writing. Over time, it was borrowed to represent the character 聿 (yù, "thereupon"), showing its evolution in meaning from a tangible object to a versatile abstract concept.
